  [[RELIGION|Hindu]]
  [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Gandhi Nagar, Ward 4]]
  [[STUDENT_ID|STU-2024-0912]]
  [[TAX_ID|29ABCDE1234F1Z5]]
  [[TELEPHONE_LANDLINE|0512-2345678]]
  [[URL|https://example.org/u/meena]]
  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]
  [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
  [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]
  [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete Referral Letter for clinical domain Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Manipuri (code=mni, script=Meitei). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Manipur, district=Imphal East.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Meitei for Manipuri.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 38
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Ordained Religious Workers, Other
- education_level: Higher Secondary/Intermediate Pre-University/Senior Secondary
- zone: Urban
- state: Manipur
- district: Imphal East
- first_language: Manipuri
- clinical_persona_summary: Male, 38, currently married, ordained religious worker with higher secondary education, living in urban Manipur, Imphal East district. Manipuri is his first language, with English proficiency. He is a disciplined community leader who manages cultural projects and attends traditional arts events. Clinically, he reports frequent worry and uses tea as a coping mechanism.

Document format guide for Referral Letter (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Referral [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-01]] from [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] / [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Re: [[PATIENT_NAME|...]], [[AGE|36]] / [[GENDER|Male]], District [[DISTRICT|...]]
Reason: <domain-appropriate clinical reason matching persona>

MANDATORY inline entities (12 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- REFERRAL_ID — Referral ID e.g. [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-1123]]

payload.generated_text_en:
Referral [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-IMPH-001]] from [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Regional Institute of Medical Sciences, Imphal]] / [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. L. Singh]]
Re: [[PATIENT_NAME|Thokchom Sanjit Singh]], [[AGE|38]] / [[GENDER|Male]], District [[DISTRICT|Imphal East]]
Reason: Persistent cough with constitutional symptoms and associated hypertension requiring specialized evaluation

Dear Colleague,

This is to refer [[PATIENT_NAME|Thokchom Sanjit Singh]], a 38-year-old male patient residing in Imphal East for further evaluation and management of suspected pulmonary tuberculosis with comorbid hypertension.

[[PATIENT_NAME|Thokchom Sanjit Singh]] presented with a 3-week history of productive cough, low-grade fever, night sweats, and weight loss of approximately 5 kg. The patient reports occasional hemoptysis and progressive dyspnea on exertion. Past medical history reveals newly diagnosed hypertension 6 months ago, currently managed with amlodipine 5mg daily. Family history is significant for diabetes mellitus in his father.

On examination, vital signs show BP 145/90 mmHg, pulse 98/min, respiratory rate 22/min, temperature 37.8°C. Chest examination reveals decreased breath sounds in the right upper zone with occasional rhonchi. Sputum examination shows numerous acid-fast bacilli on smear.

Given the clinical presentation and high TB burden in Manipur, I request your expertise for comprehensive management including drug susceptibility testing, evaluation of TB-diabetes interaction, and optimization of antihypertensive therapy.

[[PATIENT_NAME|Thokchom Sanjit Singh]] can be contacted at [[PHONE_NUMBER|9412345678]] and his [[RELATIVE_NAME|wife Thokchom Rina Devi]] at [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]. His [[MRN|MRN-IMPH-2024-0892]] and [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|A12345678]] and [[DRIVING_LICENCE|DL-MAN-2024-001234]] are available for reference.

Please coordinate with our team for smooth transition of care.

Sincerely,
[[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. L. Singh]]
[[HOSPITAL_NAME|Regional Institute of Medical Sciences, Imphal]]

payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:

GOOD:

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:

GOOD:

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):

GOOD:

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.

Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).

[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] DOB [[DOB|1994-06-21]] [[AGE|30]] [[GENDER|Female]]
Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|...]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]] Bed [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]] | Course / advice: <concise paragraphs matching domain + persona sex/age>

MANDATORY inline entities (14 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOB — Date of Birth e.g. [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- ADMISSION_NUMBER — Admission Number e.g. [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
- WARD_NUMBER — Ward Number e.g. [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]
- BED_NUMBER — Bed Number e.g. [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- ABHA_ADDRESS — ABHA Address e.g. [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
- CASTE — Caste e.g. [[CASTE|OBC]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

payload.generated_text_en:
Discharge Summary — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Regional Institute of Medical Sciences, Imphal]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Nongthombam Bimol]] DOB [[DOB|1945-08-22]] [[AGE|79]] [[GENDER|Female]]
[[MRN|IMPH-2024-0815-001]] [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0815-IMPH-001]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]] Bed [[BED_NUMBER|04]]
[[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. L. Ibohal Singh]]
[[DISTRICT|Imphal East]]

Admission Diagnosis: Pulmonary Tuberculosis with severe anemia and hypertensive crisis.
History of Present Illness: The patient, a 79-year-old widowed homemaker from Imphal East, presented with a three-week history of productive cough, intermittent low-grade fever, and profound weight loss of approximately 5 kg. She has a known history of hypertension and type 2 diabetes mellitus, which she reports are not well-controlled at home. On examination, she was cachectic, pale, and febrile with blood pressure recorded at 180/110 mmHg and random blood sugar of 280 mg/dL.

Investigations: Sputum smear microscopy was positive for Acid-Fast Bacilli (AFB). Chest X-ray revealed a cavitary lesion in the right upper lobe. Complete blood count showed hemoglobin of 6.8 g/dL. Renal and liver function tests were within acceptable limits.

Course in Hospital: The patient was initiated on Category I Anti-Tubercular Therapy (ATT) under Directly Observed Treatment Short-course (DOTS). Her hypertensive emergency was managed with intravenous labetalol and later transitioned to oral antihypertensives. Blood sugar was controlled with a sliding scale of insulin and oral hypoglycemic agents. She received two units of packed red blood cells for symptomatic anemia. Over the next 21 days, she remained afebrile, her cough frequency reduced significantly, and her appetite improved. She was hemodynamically stable on oral medications.

Discharge Condition: The patient is clinically stable and afebrile for the last 72 hours. Cough has markedly improved, and she reports increased energy levels. She is hemodynamically stable on oral antihypertensive and antidiabetic medications.

Discharge Advice: The patient is discharged with a total of six months of Anti-Tubercular Therapy, with three months remaining to be completed as DOTS. She is advised to continue her antihypertensive and antidiabetic medications regularly. A high-protein, low-salt, and low-sugar diet is recommended. She is scheduled for a follow-up at the TB clinic in one week and the Medicine OPD in two weeks. Her son, [[RELATIVE_NAME|Nongthombam Ratan]], has been thoroughly counseled regarding medication adherence, potential side effects of ATT, and the importance of follow-up visits. He was provided with the patient's [[PHONE_NUMBER|9435123456]] for any emergencies. The patient's [[ABHA_ADDRESS|nongthombam.bimol@abdm]] has been updated in the hospital records. The patient belongs to the [[CASTE|Meitei]] community.

payload.professional_persona: Kaushalyabai Jagannath, a seasoned community organiser and domestic specialist, leverages flawless budgeting, traditional cooking, event coordination and needlework to mentor younger women and sustain cultural rituals, while maintaining a disciplined routine and exacting standards.
payload.linguistic_persona: Kaushalyabai Jagannath, fluent in spoken Marathi and Khandeshi and conversational in Hindi, relies on vivid oral storytelling and mental recall to manage household tasks, community events and resolve disputes, despite being unable to read or write.
payload.sports_persona: Kaushalyabai Jagannath, who prefers gentle exercise, joins early morning walks in the local market area and attends weekly yoga sessions at the community centre, while cheering for the Mumbai Indians cricket team and occasionally playing traditional board games like Ludo with neighbours.
payload.arts_persona: Kaushalyabai Jagannath, a devoted listener of Marathi Lavani, devotional bhajans and classic Hindi films, often hums melodies of Lata Mangeshkar and Sudhir Phadke while knitting, and appreciates the theatrical performances of local Natak mandalis.
payload.travel_persona: Kaushalyabai Jagannath, who enjoys short pilgrimages to Shirdi and Pandharpur and occasional weekend getaways to Mumbai, plans trips meticulously using word of mouth and prefers traveling by train or shared auto, balancing family commitments and limited budget.
payload.culinary_persona: Kaushalyabai Jagannath, a seasoned home cook, excels at preparing poha, bhakri, puran poli and shrikhand, prefers using jaggery, kokum, fresh neem leaves and turmeric in her dishes, and enjoys savoring traditional sweets spiced with cardamom and garnished with marigold petals during festivals.
payload.persona: Kaushalyabai Jagannath, a meticulous 64‑year‑old matriarch who blends disciplined budgeting, traditional cooking and community leadership, yet battles chronic worries and occasional splurges on festival indulgences.
payload.cultural_background: Kaushalyabai belongs to a traditional Marathi‑speaking family settled in Jalna, Maharashtra, a region where Marathi culture intermingles with Khandeshi folk traditions. She was raised in a joint family that values hierarchical respect, community celebrations, and the observance of regional festivals such as Ganesh Chaturthi, Diwali, and the Khandeshi Jatra. Even though Jalna is an urban setting, her upbringing is steeped in the conventional Maharashtrian ethos of hospitality, disciplined routine, and reverence for elders.
payload.linguistic_background: As an illiterate woman, Kaushalyabai cannot read or write in any script, but she is fluent in spoken Marathi, her mother tongue, and Khandeshi, the regional dialect she uses with neighbours and relatives. She also understands basic Hindi from television and community interactions, though she relies on oral communication. Her linguistic skills are practical and centred on daily household interactions, religious rituals, and the organization of community events, where she excels in giving clear verbal instructions and remembering intricate details through mental notes.
payload.skills_and_expertise: Kaushalyabai has mastered a suite of domestic and community skills through years of hands‑on experience. She excels in preparing authentic Maharashtrian dishes such as poha, bhakri, and shrikhand, maintaining strict hygienic standards in the kitchen. She manages the household budget meticulously, using mental arithmetic and simple ledger marks despite being illiterate. Her organizational talent shines in planning family events and temple festivals, where she coordinates volunteers, prepares timelines, and ensures every ritual is performed on schedule. Additionally, she is skilled in traditional needlework and embroidery, crafts herbal remedies for common ailments, and acts as a trusted mediator in neighbourhood disputes, leveraging her assertive yet compassionate demeanor.
payload.skills_and_expertise_list: ["Maharashtrian Cooking", "Household Budget Management", "Event Planning for Family and Temple", "Traditional Needlework and Embroidery", "Herbal Remedy Preparation", "Community Mediation", "Mental Arithmetic and Record-Keeping", "Gardening and Household Organization"]
payload.hobbies_and_interests: Kaushalyabai enjoys gardening, especially tending to marigold and neem plants, knitting simple woolen shawls, listening to Marathi Lavani and devotional bhajans, preparing traditional sweets like puran poli during festivals, taking leisurely walks in the local market, attending small group discussions at the community centre, and watching classic Hindi cinema on television. She also finds pleasure in keeping a meticulous inventory of pantry staples and organizing family photo albums.
payload.hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Gardening (marigold & neem)", "Knitting woolen shawls", "Listening to Marathi Lavani and devotional bhajans", "Preparing traditional sweets (puran poli) during festivals", "Leisurely walks in the local market", "Participating in small group discussions at community centre", "Watching classic Hindi cinema on TV", "Maintaining pantry inventory and photo album organization"]
payload.career_goals_and_ambitions: Although retired, Kaushalyabai aims to continue serving her family and community by mentoring younger women in preserving traditional cooking, budgeting, and cultural rituals. She aspires to document her family's recipes and oral histories through audio recordings, ensuring they are passed down to future generations. She also seeks to strengthen the local women's self‑help group by organizing skill‑sharing workshops, thereby fostering financial independence and social support for the neighbourhood, while maintaining her own mental well‑being through a disciplined daily routine.
payload.persona_clinical_summary: A 64-year-old female, currently married, retired homemaker from Maharashtra, Jalna, with a Marathi-speaking background. She is illiterate but fluent in spoken Marathi and understands basic Hindi. Clinically, she has a history of chronic worry and occasional dietary splurges during festivals.
payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete Referral Letter for clinical domain Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Marathi (code=mr, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Maharashtra, district=Jalna.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Devanagari for Marathi.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 64
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Illiterate
- zone: Urban
- state: Maharashtra
- district: Jalna
- first_language: Marathi
- clinical_persona_summary: A 64-year-old female, currently married, retired homemaker from Maharashtra, Jalna, with a Marathi-speaking background. She is illiterate but fluent in spoken Marathi and understands basic Hindi. Clinically, she has a history of chronic worry and occasional dietary splurges during festivals.

Document format guide for Referral Letter (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Referral [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-01]] from [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] / [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Re: [[PATIENT_NAME|...]], [[AGE|36]] / [[GENDER|Male]], District [[DISTRICT|...]]
Reason: <domain-appropriate clinical reason matching persona>

MANDATORY inline entities (12 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- REFERRAL_ID — Referral ID e.g. [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-1123]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- PASSPORT_NUMBER — Passport Number e.g. [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|Z1234567]]
- DRIVING_LICENCE — Driving Licence Number e.g. [[DRIVING_LICENCE|UP14 20110012345]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

संदर्भ पत्र [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-JLN-001]] कडून [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Jalna Urban Primary Health Centre]] / [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajesh Deshmukh]]
विषय: [[PATIENT_NAME|Savitri Bai Patil]], [[AGE|64]] / [[GENDER|Female]], जिल्हा [[DISTRICT|Jalna]]
कारण: उपचार सुरू असलेल्या ज्ञात फुफ्फुसीय क्षयरोग (पल्मोनरी ट्युबरक्युलोसिस) असलेल्या रुग्णाबद्दल, आता अनियंत्रित उच्च रक्तदाब आणि नव्याने निदान झालेला मधुमेहाचा प्रकार (डायबिटीज मेलिटस) दिसून येत आहे.

सध्याच्या आजाराचा इतिहास:
रुग्ण 64 वर्षांची गृहिणी असून, तिला 3 महिन्यांपूर्वी निदान झालेला फुफ्फुसीय क्षयरोग (पल्मोनरी ट्युबरक्युलोसिस) असल्याचे ज्ञात आहे. ती कॅटेगरी I डीओटीएस थेरपीवर आहे. गेल्या दोन आठवड्यांपासून, तिला सतत डोकेदुखी, चक्कर येणे आणि वाढलेली थकवा जाणवत आहे. तिला वारंवार लघवी होणे (पॉलीयुरिया) आणि तहान लागणे (पॉलिडिप्सिया) यांसारख्या तक्रारी आहेत.

पूर्वीचा वैद्यकीय इतिहास:
फुफ्फुसीय क्षयरोग (3 महिन्यांपूर्वी निदान झालेला). उच्च रक्तदाब किंवा मधुमेहाचा कोणताही ज्ञात इतिहास नाही.

तपासणीतील निष्कर्ष:
तपासणीत, तिची जीवनचिन्हे (व्हाइटल्स) स्थिर आहेत. रक्तदाब 160/100 mmHg नोंदवला गेला आहे. सामान्य तपासणीत काहीही असामान्य आढळले नाही.

तपासण्या:
यादृच्छिक रक्त शर्करा (रँडम ब्लड शुगर) 250 mg/dL आढळली. एएफबीसाठी थुंकीची तपासणी नकारात्मक आहे. छातीचा क्ष-किरण (चेस्ट एक्स-रे) अहवाल सुधारणारे इन्फिल्ट्रेट्स दर्शवतो.

मूल्यमापन आणि योजना:
मूल्यमापन:
1. फुफ्फुसीय क्षयरोग, उपचार टप्पा 2.
2. नव्याने उद्भवलेला उच्च रक्तदाब.
3. नव्याने उद्भवलेला मधुमेहाचा प्रकार (डायबिटीज मेलिटस).

रुग्णाला तिच्या नव्याने निदान झालेल्या असंसर्गजन्य आजारांसाठी तज्ज्ञांकडून मूल्यमापन आणि व्यवस्थापनाची आवश्यकता आहे. आम्ही सविस्तर तपासणी आणि उच्च रक्तदाबविरोधी व मधुमेहाविरोधी उपचारांच्या सुरुवातीची विनंती करतो, तसेच तिच्या सुरू असलेल्या क्षयरोगविरोधी औषधांशी होणाऱ्या संभाव्य औषध-औषध परस्परसंवादावर (ड्रग-ड्रग इंटरअॅक्शन) बारकाईने लक्ष ठेवण्याची विनंती करतो. कृपया सल्ला द्यावा.

कोणत्याही पुढील माहितीसाठी, कृपया रुग्णाच्या मुलाशी संपर्क साधावा, [[RELATIVE_NAME|Vikram Patil]], या क्रमांकावर [[PHONE_NUMBER|9822012345]]. रुग्णाची आयडी तपशील: [[MRN|MRN-JLN-2024-06401]], [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|P7894561]], [[DRIVING_LICENCE|MH02 20100012345]].
payload.generated_text_en:
Referral [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-JLN-001]] from [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Jalna Urban Primary Health Centre]] / [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajesh Deshmukh]]
Re: [[PATIENT_NAME|Savitri Bai Patil]], [[AGE|64]] / [[GENDER|Female]], District [[DISTRICT|Jalna]]
Reason: Patient with known pulmonary Tuberculosis on treatment, now presenting with uncontrolled hypertension and newly diagnosed diabetes mellitus.

History of Present Illness:
The patient, a 64-year-old female homemaker, is a known case of pulmonary TB diagnosed 3 months ago. She has been on Category I DOTS therapy. Over the past two weeks, she reports persistent headaches, dizziness, and increased fatigue. She also complains of polyuria and polydipsia.

Past Medical History:
History of Pulmonary Tuberculosis (diagnosed 3 months ago). No known history of hypertension or diabetes.

Examination Findings:
On examination, her vitals are stable. Blood pressure is recorded at 160/100 mmHg. General examination is unremarkable.

Investigations:
Random blood sugar was found to be 250 mg/dL. Sputum for AFB is negative. Chest X-ray shows resolving infiltrates.

Assessment and Plan:
Assessment: 1. Pulmonary Tuberculosis, treatment phase 2. New-onset Hypertension 3. New-onset Diabetes Mellitus.
Plan: The patient requires specialist evaluation and management for her newly diagnosed Non-Communicable Diseases. We request a detailed workup and initiation of antihypertensive and antidiabetic therapy, with close monitoring for potential drug-drug interactions with her ongoing anti-tubercular medication. Please advise.

For any further information, please contact the patient's son, [[RELATIVE_NAME|Vikram Patil]], at [[PHONE_NUMBER|9822012345]]. Patient's ID details: [[MRN|MRN-JLN-2024-06401]], [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|P7894561]], [[DRIVING_LICENCE|MH02 20100012345]].

payload.persona: Kaushalyabai Jagannath, a meticulous 64‑year‑old matriarch who blends disciplined budgeting, traditional cooking and community leadership, yet battles chronic worries and occasional splurges on festival indulgences.
payload.cultural_background: Kaushalyabai belongs to a traditional Marathi‑speaking family settled in Jalna, Maharashtra, a region where Marathi culture intermingles with Khandeshi folk traditions. She was raised in a joint family that values hierarchical respect, community celebrations, and the observance of regional festivals such as Ganesh Chaturthi, Diwali, and the Khandeshi Jatra. Even though Jalna is an urban setting, her upbringing is steeped in the conventional Maharashtrian ethos of hospitality, disciplined routine, and reverence for elders.
payload.linguistic_background: As an illiterate woman, Kaushalyabai cannot read or write in any script, but she is fluent in spoken Marathi, her mother tongue, and Khandeshi, the regional dialect she uses with neighbours and relatives. She also understands basic Hindi from television and community interactions, though she relies on oral communication. Her linguistic skills are practical and centred on daily household interactions, religious rituals, and the organization of community events, where she excels in giving clear verbal instructions and remembering intricate details through mental notes.
payload.skills_and_expertise: Kaushalyabai has mastered a suite of domestic and community skills through years of hands‑on experience. She excels in preparing authentic Maharashtrian dishes such as poha, bhakri, and shrikhand, maintaining strict hygienic standards in the kitchen. She manages the household budget meticulously, using mental arithmetic and simple ledger marks despite being illiterate. Her organizational talent shines in planning family events and temple festivals, where she coordinates volunteers, prepares timelines, and ensures every ritual is performed on schedule. Additionally, she is skilled in traditional needlework and embroidery, crafts herbal remedies for common ailments, and acts as a trusted mediator in neighbourhood disputes, leveraging her assertive yet compassionate demeanor.
payload.skills_and_expertise_list: ["Maharashtrian Cooking", "Household Budget Management", "Event Planning for Family and Temple", "Traditional Needlework and Embroidery", "Herbal Remedy Preparation", "Community Mediation", "Mental Arithmetic and Record-Keeping", "Gardening and Household Organization"]
payload.hobbies_and_interests: Kaushalyabai enjoys gardening, especially tending to marigold and neem plants, knitting simple woolen shawls, listening to Marathi Lavani and devotional bhajans, preparing traditional sweets like puran poli during festivals, taking leisurely walks in the local market, attending small group discussions at the community centre, and watching classic Hindi cinema on television. She also finds pleasure in keeping a meticulous inventory of pantry staples and organizing family photo albums.
payload.hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Gardening (marigold & neem)", "Knitting woolen shawls", "Listening to Marathi Lavani and devotional bhajans", "Preparing traditional sweets (puran poli) during festivals", "Leisurely walks in the local market", "Participating in small group discussions at community centre", "Watching classic Hindi cinema on TV", "Maintaining pantry inventory and photo album organization"]
payload.career_goals_and_ambitions: Although retired, Kaushalyabai aims to continue serving her family and community by mentoring younger women in preserving traditional cooking, budgeting, and cultural rituals. She aspires to document her family's recipes and oral histories through audio recordings, ensuring they are passed down to future generations. She also seeks to strengthen the local women's self‑help group by organizing skill‑sharing workshops, thereby fostering financial independence and social support for the neighbourhood, while maintaining her own mental well‑being through a disciplined daily routine.
payload.persona_clinical_summary: A 64-year-old female, currently married, retired homemaker with illiterate education, residing in an urban area of Maharashtra. She is a Marathi-speaking woman from Jalna district, who manages household budgeting and traditional cooking. Clinically, she may present with stress-related symptoms and occasional dietary indiscretions during festivals.
payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):
  [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
  [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]
  [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
  [[AGE|34]]
  [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]
  [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|Sunita Yadav]]
  [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|50200012345678]]
  [[BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER|SBIN0001234]]
  [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
  [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-UP-208001-4421]]
  [[CASTE|OBC]]
  [[CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER|4111111111111111]]
  [[CVV|123]]
  [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
  [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
  [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
  [[DRIVING_LICENCE|UP14 20110012345]]
  [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|meena.devi@example.com]]
  [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]
  [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
  [[GENDER|Female]]
  [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
  [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]]
  [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
  [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
  [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]]
  [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]]
  [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
  [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]]
  [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]]
  [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|Z1234567]]
  [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]
  [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
  [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
  [[PIN|4829]]
  [[PIN_CODE|208001]]
  [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-1123]]
  [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
  [[RELIGION|Hindu]]
  [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Gandhi Nagar, Ward 4]]
  [[STUDENT_ID|STU-2024-0912]]
  [[TAX_ID|29ABCDE1234F1Z5]]
  [[TELEPHONE_LANDLINE|0512-2345678]]
  [[URL|https://example.org/u/meena]]
  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]
  [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
  [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]
  [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete ER Triage & Nursing Notes for clinical domain Surgical.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Marathi (code=mr, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Surgical — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Maharashtra, district=Jalna.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Devanagari for Marathi.

PLACEMENT (vehicle): Put VEHICLE_REGISTRATION exactly once in the arrival/RTA/ambulance line. Do not dump or repeat it.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 64
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Illiterate
- zone: Urban
- state: Maharashtra
- district: Jalna
- first_language: Marathi
- clinical_persona_summary: A 64-year-old female, currently married, retired homemaker with illiterate education, residing in an urban area of Maharashtra. She is a Marathi-speaking woman from Jalna district, who manages household budgeting and traditional cooking. Clinically, she may present with stress-related symptoms and occasional dietary indiscretions during festivals.

Document format guide for ER Triage & Nursing Notes (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
ER triage — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] Encounter [[ENCOUNTER_ID|...]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|22]] [[GENDER|Male]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|ER]] Bed [[BED_NUMBER|...]]
Arrived by ambulance / RTA vehicle [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|PB10AB1234]] (exactly once).
Relative [[RELATIVE_NAME|...]] Phone [[PHONE_NUMBER|...]] Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Vitals / acuity: <short notes>

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- ENCOUNTER_ID — Encounter / Visit ID e.g. [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
- WARD_NUMBER — Ward Number e.g. [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- BED_NUMBER — Bed Number e.g. [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION — Vehicle Registration Number e.g. [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
payload.generated_text:
इमर्जन्सी ट्रायज — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Jalna District Hospital]] भेट [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-2024-0815-001]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Savitri Bai Patil]] [[AGE|64]] [[GENDER|Female]] वॉर्ड [[WARD_NUMBER|ER]] बेड [[BED_NUMBER|05]]
ॲम्ब्युलन्सने आलेले [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|MH12AB1234]]
नातेवाईक [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Patil]] फोन [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]] डॉ. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajesh Deshmukh]]
व्हायटल्स / तीव्रता: रुग्ण शुद्धीवर, सतर्क, मध्यम त्रासात आहे. BP 140/90, HR 110, RR 22, Temp 38.5°C, SpO2 96% सामान्य हवेवर. उजव्या खालच्या पोटाच्या भागात कोमलता आणि स्नायूंचा ताण.

नर्सिंग नोट्स: रुग्ण सांगतो की जड जेवणानंतर 6 तास आधी अचानक पोटदुखी सुरू झाली. वेदना उजव्या खांद्यापर्यंत जातात. मळमळ आणि उलट्या x2 वेळा. पूर्वी ताप नव्हता. शेवटची शौचक्रिया 2 दिवसांपूर्वी झाली. पूर्वीची शस्त्रक्रिया इतिहास: काहीही नाही. ॲलर्जी: माहित नाही. 18G कॅन्युलासह डाव्या हातावर IV ॲक्सेस तयार केला. 1L नॉर्मल सलाईन बोलस सुरू केला. वेदना स्कोअर 7/10. Morphine 4mg IV दिले. रुग्ण शस्त्रक्रिया तज्ज्ञाच्या सल्ल्याची वाट पाहत आहे.

योजना: आणीबाणीची अपेंडिक्स शस्त्रक्रिया होण्याची शक्यता आहे. नातेवाईकाकडून संमती घेतली. ऑपरेशनपूर्वीचे लॅब्स ऑर्डर केले. NPO स्थिती राखली. व्हायटल्स दर 15 मिनिटांनी तपासा. OR ट्रान्सफरसाठी तयार राहा.
payload.generated_text_en:
ER triage — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Jalna District Hospital]] Encounter [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-2024-0815-001]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Savitri Bai Patil]] [[AGE|64]] [[GENDER|Female]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|ER]] Bed [[BED_NUMBER|05]]
Arrived by ambulance vehicle [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|MH12AB1234]]
Relative [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Patil]] Phone [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]] Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajesh Deshmukh]]
Vitals / acuity: Patient conscious, alert, in moderate distress. BP 140/90, HR 110, RR 22, Temp 38.5°C, SpO2 96% on room air. Abdominal tenderness in right lower quadrant with guarding.

Nursing notes: Patient reports sudden onset abdominal pain 6 hours ago after heavy meal. Pain radiates to right shoulder. Nausea and vomiting x2 episodes. No fever history. Last bowel movement 2 days ago. Past surgical history: none. Allergies: none known. IV access established in left forearm with 18G cannula. 1L normal saline bolus initiated. Pain score 7/10. Morphine 4mg IV administered. Patient awaiting surgical consult.

Plan: Emergency appendectomy likely. Consent obtained from relative. Pre-operative labs ordered. NPO status maintained. Monitor vitals q15min. Prepare for OR transfer.

She also seeks to strengthen the local women's self‑help group by organizing skill‑sharing workshops, thereby fostering financial independence and social support for the neighbourhood, while maintaining her own mental well‑being through a disciplined daily routine.
payload.persona_clinical_summary: A 64-year-old female from Maharashtra, currently married, with no formal occupation and illiteracy. She is an urban resident who speaks Marathi and Khandeshi, and her lifestyle includes traditional cooking, community leadership, and disciplined budgeting. Clinically, she may present with chronic stress and occasional dietary indiscretions during festivals.
payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):

  [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

  [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
  [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
  [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
  [[DRIVING_LICENCE|UP14 20110012345]]
  [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|meena.devi@example.com]]
  [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]
  [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
  [[GENDER|Female]]
  [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
  [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]]
  [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
  [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]

  [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]]

  [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]]

  [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Gandhi Nagar, Ward 4]]
  [[STUDENT_ID|STU-2024-0912]]
  [[TAX_ID|29ABCDE1234F1Z5]]
  [[TELEPHONE_LANDLINE|0512-2345678]]
  [[URL|https://example.org/u/meena]]
  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]
  [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
  [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]
  [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete Radiology / Imaging Report for clinical domain General Medicine.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Marathi (code=mr, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match General Medicine — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Maharashtra, district=Jalna.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Devanagari for Marathi.

PLACEMENT: Include [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] as the reporting radiologist (e.g. Reported by Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]]) — do not leave the doctor name as untagged prose.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 64
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Illiterate
- zone: Urban
- state: Maharashtra
- district: Jalna
- first_language: Marathi
- clinical_persona_summary: A 64-year-old female from Maharashtra, currently married, with no formal occupation and illiteracy. She is an urban resident who speaks Marathi and Khandeshi, and her lifestyle includes traditional cooking, community leadership, and disciplined budgeting. Clinically, she may present with chronic stress and occasional dietary indiscretions during festivals.

Document format guide for Radiology / Imaging Report (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Imaging report — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] | [[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|45]] [[GENDER|Female]]
MRN [[MRN|...]] Encounter [[ENCOUNTER_ID|...]] | Reported by Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Findings: <radiology prose, untagged>

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- ENCOUNTER_ID — Encounter / Visit ID e.g. [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- HOSPITAL_ID — Hospital ID e.g. [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
- ABHA_ID — ABHA ID e.g. [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

रेडिओलॉजी रिपोर्ट — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Jalna District Hospital]] | [[PATIENT_NAME|Savitri Patil]] [[AGE|64]] [[GENDER|Female]]
MRN [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]] भेट [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]] | डॉ. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajesh Deshmukh]] यांनी कळवले आहे
निष्कर्ष: [[PATIENT_NAME|Savitri Patil]] वर केलेला पोटाचा अल्ट्रासाऊंड सौम्य हिपॅटोमेगाली दर्शवतो, ज्यामध्ये यकृताची लांबी 16.5 सेमी मोजली गेली आहे. पित्ताशयात अनेक लहान खडे (3-5 मिमी) दिसतात. प्लीहा आकाराने सामान्य दिसते. मूत्रपिंड दोन्ही बाजूंनी सौम्य वाढलेले असून इकोजेनिसिटी कायम आहे. हायड्रोनेफ्रोसिस आढळले नाही. स्वादुपिंड सामान्य दिसते. आतड्यांचे लूप्स सामान्य पेरिस्टॅलिसिस दर्शवतात. मुक्त द्रव किंवा जलोदर आढळले नाही. पित्ताशयाची भिंत जाड झाली आहे. सामायिक पित्तवाहिनीचे माप 6 मिमी आहे. कोणतेही फोकल हिपॅटिक लीजन्स आढळले नाहीत. यकृताच्या रक्तवाहिन्यांमध्ये डॉप्लर प्रवाह सामान्य दिसतो.

प्रभाव: 1. सौम्य हिपॅटोमेगालीसह पित्ताशयातील खडे 2. दोन्ही मूत्रपिंडांची वाढ 3. पित्ताशयाची भिंत जाड होणे

शिफारस: क्लिनिकल कोरिलेशन आणि पुढील व्यवस्थापनासाठी [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajesh Deshmukh]] यांच्याकडे पाठपुरावा करा. भेटीची वेळ निश्चित करण्यासाठी [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]] शी संपर्क साधा.

[[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-MH-018]] | [[DISTRICT|Jalna]] | [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]
payload.generated_text_en:
Radiology Report — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Jalna District Hospital]] | [[PATIENT_NAME|Savitri Patil]] [[AGE|64]] [[GENDER|Female]]
MRN [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]] Encounter [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]] | Reported by Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajesh Deshmukh]]
Findings: Abdominal ultrasound performed on [[PATIENT_NAME|Savitri Patil]] shows mild hepatomegaly with liver span measuring 16.5 cm. Gallbladder is visualized with multiple small calculi measuring 3-5mm. Spleen appears normal in size. Kidneys are mildly enlarged bilaterally with preserved echogenicity. No hydronephrosis noted. Pancreas appears normal. Bowel loops show normal peristalsis. No free fluid or ascites detected. Gallbladder wall is thickened. Common bile duct measures 6mm. No focal hepatic lesions identified. Doppler flow in hepatic vasculature appears normal.

Impression: 1. Mild hepatomegaly with cholelithiasis 2. Bilateral renal enlargement 3. Gallbladder wall thickening

Recommendation: Follow-up with [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajesh Deshmukh]] for clinical correlation and further management. Contact [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]] for appointment scheduling.

[[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-MH-018]] | [[DISTRICT|Jalna]] | [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

payload.sports_persona: Geeta Das enjoys early‑morning walks through tea‑garden trails and follows football passionately, cheering for Mohun Bagan and supporting the local Jalpaiguri cricket club, while they occasionally join a community yoga group for flexibility.
payload.arts_persona: Geeta Das immerses themselves in local folk music, often humming the melodies of Nepali legends Narayan Gopal and Raju Pariyar, while they craft clay figurines inspired by traditional puppetry and paint vivid scenes of forest life reminiscent of Amrita Sher‑Gill’s brushwork.
payload.travel_persona: Geeta Das prefers modest, self‑planned treks to nearby hill stations like Darjeeling and the tea‑garden trails of Kalimpong, while they dream of a cross‑border Himalayan pilgrimage to Nepal’s Annapurna region, and they balance their love for nature with the need to keep travel costs low for their family.
payload.culinary_persona: Geeta Das enjoys hearty dal‑bhat with gundruk leaves, spicy Nepali momo filled with minced chicken and garlic, and tea flavored with cardamom and ginger, though they prefer buying ready‑made snacks from the local market rather than cooking elaborate meals.
payload.persona: Geeta Das is a methodical forest guardian whose curiosity for folk art fuels competitive mentorship, who balances cash‑only spending with modest gold savings, and who, despite being illiterate, they navigate daily life through oral tradition while occasionally forgetting follow‑up health checks.
payload.cultural_background: Geeta belongs to the Nepali‑speaking Gorkha community in the tea‑garden belt of Jalpaiguri, West Bengal. Her cultural upbringing blends traditional Hindu customs from the hills with the rural Bengali milieu, celebrating festivals such as Dashain, Tihar, and Durga Puja, and preserving local folk songs and crafts.
payload.linguistic_background: Geeta is illiterate but speaks Nepali fluently, which is her mother tongue. She can communicate effectively in Nepali for daily tasks and forest work. Through community interaction she has functional comprehension of Hindi and Bengali, allowing her to follow simple instructions and engage in informal conversations, though she cannot read or write in any script.
payload.skills_and_expertise: Geeta has practical expertise in forest management, including tree planting, sapling care, trail maintenance, fire prevention, and community coordination for forest conservation. She is adept at using basic surveying tools, identifying native tree species, and applying traditional knowledge of medicinal plants. Her methodical approach ensures systematic record‑keeping through oral logs.

payload.hobbies_and_interests: Geeta has a deep interest in art, especially local folk music and handicrafts. She enjoys trekking in the forest, identifying flowers and herbs, gardening, and listening to traditional Nepali songs. In her free time she makes small clay figures, paints, and participates in village festivals.
payload.hobbies_and_interests_list: ['folk music listening', 'handicraft making', 'trekking in forests', 'gardening', 'plant identification', 'traditional Nepali cooking', 'painting', 'community festivals', 'storytelling']
payload.career_goals_and_ambitions: Geeta aspires to become a senior forester and lead community‑driven conservation projects in the Jalpaiguri forest region. She aims to acquire informal training in sustainable forestry, improve fire management practices, and mentor younger women from her community. She also wishes to document local folk art tied to forest culture, hoping to blend her curiosity for art with her professional role.
payload.persona_clinical_summary: Ms. Geeta Das, 48, is a female forester in rural West Bengal, currently married, with an illiterate education level. She resides in Jalpaiguri district and speaks Nepali as her first language, with functional Hindi and Bengali. Her lifestyle includes cash-only spending and modest gold savings.
