  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
payload.user_prompt:

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Punjabi (code=pa, script=Gurmukhi). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Punjab, district=Moga.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Gurmukhi for Punjabi.

PLACEMENT (OPD header): First line MUST include BOTH [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] (facility name) AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] (facility code) — never substitute one for the other.
Put [[EMPLOYEE_ID|…]] on the registrar/clerk line and [[RELATIVE_NAME|…]] on the attendant/relative line. Include [[DOB|YYYY-MM-DD]] and [[OCCUPATION|…]] with demographics.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 18
- marital_status: Never Married
- occupation: Stall and Market Salespersons, Other
- education_level: Matric/Secondary
- zone: Urban
- state: Punjab
- district: Moga
- first_language: Punjabi
- clinical_persona_summary: Salkhan Varma is an 18‑year‑old male from Moga, Punjab, who works as a market stall salesperson with a matriculation education. He is never married, lives in an urban setting, and speaks Punjabi as his first language with functional Hindi. His lifestyle includes daily runs, occasional gym sessions, and a preference for budget‑friendly trips to places like Shimla.

Document format guide for OPD Slip (Outpatient Department) (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton (adapt language/script; keep tags):
OPD Slip | [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital X]] | ID [[HOSPITAL_ID|DH-X-01]]
Patient: [[PATIENT_NAME|...]] | DOB [[DOB|1992-03-14]] | Age: [[AGE|34]] | Gender: [[GENDER|Female]]
Occupation: [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]] | MRN: [[MRN|OPD-2024-001]] | Doctor: [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. ...]]
Relative: [[RELATIVE_NAME|...]] | Phone: [[PHONE_NUMBER|98XXXXXXXX]]
Registrar EmpID: [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-4412]] | District: [[DISTRICT|...]]
Chief complaint: <short clinical line matching assigned domain>

MANDATORY inline entities (13 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOB — Date of Birth e.g. [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
- OCCUPATION — Occupation e.g. [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- HOSPITAL_ID — Hospital ID e.g. [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- EMPLOYEE_ID — Employee ID e.g. [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ਮੁੱਖ ਸ਼ਿਕਾਇਤ: ਲਗਾਤਾਰ ਖੰਘ 3 ਹਫ਼ਤਿਆਂ ਤੋਂ ਭਾਰ ਘਟਣ ਅਤੇ ਕਦੇ-ਕਦਾਈਂ ਬੁਖਾਰ ਦੇ ਨਾਲ।

ਮੌਜੂਦਾ ਬਿਮਾਰੀ ਦਾ ਇਤਿਹਾਸ: ਮਰੀਜ਼ 21 ਦਿਨਾਂ ਤੋਂ ਬਲਗਮ ਵਾਲੀ ਖੰਘ ਨਾਲ ਪੇਸ਼ ਕਰਦਾ ਹੈ, ਜੋ ਸ਼ੁਰੂ ਵਿੱਚ ਖੁਸ਼ਕ ਸੀ ਅਤੇ ਫਿਰ ਪੀਲੀ ਬਲਗਮ ਦੇ ਨਾਲ ਬਲਗਮ ਵਾਲੀ ਹੋ ਗਈ। ਮਰੀਜ਼ ਸ਼ਾਮ ਨੂੰ ਹਲਕਾ ਬੁਖਾਰ, ਰਾਤ ਨੂੰ ਪਸੀਨਾ ਆਉਣ ਅਤੇ ਪਿਛਲੇ ਮਹੀਨੇ ਵਿੱਚ 5 ਕਿਲੋ ਭਾਰ ਘਟਣ ਦੀ ਰਿਪੋਰਟ ਕਰਦਾ ਹੈ। TB ਮਰੀਜ਼ਾਂ ਨਾਲ ਕੋਈ ਜਾਣਿਆ-ਪਛਾਣਿਆ ਸੰਪਰਕ ਨਹੀਂ ਹੈ।

ਜਾਂਚ ਦੇ ਨਤੀਜੇ: ਤਾਪਮਾਨ 37.8°C, ਨਾੜੀ 92/ਮਿੰਟ, ਸਾਹ ਦੀ ਦਰ 18/ਮਿੰਟ, ਬਲੱਡ ਪ੍ਰੈਸ਼ਰ 110/70 mmHg। ਛਾਤੀ ਦੀ ਜਾਂਚ ਵਿੱਚ ਸੱਜੇ ਉੱਪਰਲੇ ਹਿੱਸੇ ਵਿੱਚ ਕਦੇ-ਕਦਾਈਂ ਰੌਂਕੀ ਦੇ ਨਾਲ ਸਾਹ ਦੀਆਂ ਆਵਾਜ਼ਾਂ ਵਿੱਚ ਕਮੀ ਦੇਖੀ ਗਈ। ਕੋਈ ਕਲੱਬਿੰਗ ਨਹੀਂ ਦੇਖੀ ਗਈ।

ਜਾਂਚਾਂ: AFB ਸਮੇਅਰ ਲਈ ਬਲਗਮ ਪਾਜ਼ੇਟਿਵ (2+), Mycobacterium tuberculosis ਲਈ GeneXpert MTB/RIF ਪਾਜ਼ੇਟਿਵ, ESR 45 mm/ਘੰਟਾ, Hb 11.2 g/dL। ਛਾਤੀ ਦਾ ਐਕਸ-ਰੇਅ ਸੱਜੇ ਉੱਪਰਲੇ ਲੋਬ ਵਿੱਚ ਇਨਫਿਲਟਰੇਟਸ ਦਿਖਾਉਂਦਾ ਹੈ।

ਨਿਦਾਨ: ਫੇਫੜਿਆਂ ਦੀ ਤਪਦਿਕ, ਨਵਾਂ ਨਿਦਾਨ ਕੀਤਾ ਗਿਆ, ਫੇਫੜਿਆਂ ਦਾ ਰੂਪ

ਇਲਾਜ ਯੋਜਨਾ: (Isoniazid, Rifampicin, Pyrazinamide, Ethambutol) ਦੇ 4-ਦਵਾਈਆਂ ਦੇ ਕੋਰਸ 'ਤੇ RNTCP ਦਿਸ਼ਾ-ਨਿਰਦੇਸ਼ਾਂ ਅਨੁਸਾਰ ਸ਼ੁਰੂ ਕੀਤਾ ਗਿਆ। ਮਰੀਜ਼ ਨੂੰ ਇਲਾਜ ਦੀ ਪਾਲਣਾ, ਮਾੜੇ ਪ੍ਰਭਾਵਾਂ ਦੀ ਨਿਗਰਾਨੀ ਅਤੇ ਲਾਗ ਨਿਯੰਤਰਣ ਦੇ ਉਪਾਵਾਂ ਬਾਰੇ ਸਲਾਹ ਦਿੱਤੀ ਗਈ। 2 ਹਫ਼ਤਿਆਂ ਵਿੱਚ ਬਲਗਮ ਦੇ ਮੁੜ-ਮੁਲਾਂਕਣ ਲਈ ਫਾਲੋ-ਅਪ ਨਿਰਧਾਰਤ ਕੀਤਾ ਗਿਆ ਹੈ। ਪੋਸ਼ਣ ਸੰਬੰਧੀ ਸਲਾਹ ਦਿੱਤੀ ਗਈ।
payload.generated_text_en:
OPD Slip | [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Moga]] | ID [[HOSPITAL_ID|DH-MOG-001]]
Patient: [[PATIENT_NAME|Salkhan Varma]] | DOB [[DOB|2006-05-15]] | Age: [[AGE|18]] | Gender: [[GENDER|Male]]
Occupation: [[OCCUPATION|Stall and Market Salespersons, Other]] | MRN: [[MRN|OPD-2024-1023]] | Doctor: [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajinder Singh]]
Relative: [[RELATIVE_NAME|Balwinder Varma]] | Phone: [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]
Registrar EmpID: [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-2024-015]] | District: [[DISTRICT|Moga]]
Chief complaint: Persistent cough for 3 weeks with weight loss and occasional fever

History of present illness: Patient presents with productive cough for 21 days, initially dry then becoming productive with yellow sputum. Reports low-grade fever in evenings, night sweats, and 5kg weight loss over past month. No known contact with TB patients.

Examination findings: Temperature 37.8°C, Pulse 92/min, Respiratory rate 18/min, BP 110/70 mmHg. Chest examination shows decreased breath sounds in right upper zone with occasional rhonchi. No clubbing noted.

Investigations: Sputum for AFB smear positive (2+), GeneXpert MTB/RIF positive for Mycobacterium tuberculosis, ESR 45 mm/hr, Hb 11.2 g/dL. Chest X-ray shows infiltrates in right upper lobe.

Diagnosis: Pulmonary Tuberculosis, newly diagnosed, pulmonary form

Treatment plan: Initiated on 4-drug regimen (Isoniazid, Rifampicin, Pyrazinamide, Ethambutol) as per RNTCP guidelines. Patient counseled on treatment adherence, side effects monitoring, and infection control measures. Follow-up scheduled in 2 weeks for sputum re-evaluation. Nutritional advice provided.

payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.

Occupation: [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]] | MRN: [[MRN|OPD-2024-001]] | Doctor: [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. ...]]

Registrar EmpID: [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-4412]] | District: [[DISTRICT|...]]

MANDATORY inline entities (13 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:

- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOB — Date of Birth e.g. [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
- OCCUPATION — Occupation e.g. [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- HOSPITAL_ID — Hospital ID e.g. [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- EMPLOYEE_ID — Employee ID e.g. [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

OPD Slip | [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Sher-i-Kashmir Institute of Medical Sciences]] | ID [[HOSPITAL_ID|SKIMS-SGR-001]]
Patient: [[PATIENT_NAME|Jammer Shamar]] | DOB [[DOB|1996-05-15]] | Age: [[AGE|28]] | Gender: [[GENDER|Male]]
Occupation: [[OCCUPATION|Keypunch Operator]] | MRN: [[MRN|OPD-2024-0892-001]] | Doctor: [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Nayeem Ahmad]]

Chief complaint: Persistent dry cough for 3 weeks with occasional blood-tinged sputum, low-grade evening fever, and 5kg weight loss. Patient also reports mild breathlessness on exertion and occasional chest discomfort.

History of Present Illness: Patient is a 28-year-old male keypunch operator presenting with progressive cough for 3 weeks. Initially dry cough, now productive with occasional blood-tinged sputum. Associated symptoms include evening low-grade fever (99-100°F), night sweats, and significant weight loss of 5kg over 2 months. Reports mild dyspnea on climbing stairs. No known sick contacts.

Past Medical History: Diagnosed with hypertension 6 months ago, on regular medication. No known diabetes or cardiac disease. No previous TB exposure or treatment.

Physical Examination:
- General: Thin build, conscious, oriented, mildly anxious
- Vital signs: BP 140/90 mmHg, Pulse 88/min, Temp 99.2°F, RR 18/min, SpO2 94% on room air
- Chest: Bilateral breath sounds present, occasional rhonchi in right upper zone, no wheezing or crepitations
- Other systems: Within normal limits

Investigations Ordered:
- Sputum for AFB smear and culture
- Chest X-ray PA view
- Complete blood count
- ESR and CRP
- Blood sugar fasting and post-prandial
- ECG

Provisional Diagnosis:
1. Pulmonary Tuberculosis (RNTCP Category I)
2. Essential Hypertension

Treatment Plan:
- Start anti-tubercular therapy as per RNTCP guidelines
- Continue antihypertensive medication
- Nutritional supplementation
- Follow-up in 2 weeks for review of investigations and treatment response

Patient counseled regarding TB treatment adherence, importance of completing full course, and side effects monitoring. Advised to report any adverse effects immediately.

payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete Prescription for clinical domain General Medicine.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Punjabi (code=pa, script=Gurmukhi). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match General Medicine — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Punjab, district=Moga.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Gurmukhi for Punjabi.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 18
- marital_status: Never Married
- occupation: Stall and Market Salespersons, Other
- education_level: Matric/Secondary
- zone: Urban
- state: Punjab
- district: Moga
- first_language: Punjabi
- clinical_persona_summary: A pragmatic 18‑year‑old male from Moga, Punjab, works as a market salesperson with a matriculation education and lives in an urban setting. He is never married, speaks Punjabi as his first language, and has functional Hindi proficiency with limited English. His lifestyle includes late‑night comic reading and a love for Bhangra music, while he saves money from his stall earnings.

Document format guide for Prescription (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Prescription — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]]
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|...]], [[AGE|28]] / [[GENDER|Male]], MRN [[MRN|RX-1001]]
Dr. [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Rx: <drug dose frequency>  (do NOT tag drug names as entities)

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS — Residential Address e.g. [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Gandhi Nagar, Ward 4]]
- PATIENT_ID — Patient ID e.g. [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]
- ABHA_ID — ABHA ID e.g. [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).

ਮਰੀਜ਼ ਪਿਛਲੇ ਦੋ ਹਫ਼ਤਿਆਂ ਤੋਂ ਆਮ ਕਮਜ਼ੋਰੀ ਅਤੇ ਥਕਾਵਟ ਦੀਆਂ ਸ਼ਿਕਾਇਤਾਂ ਨਾਲ ਪੇਸ਼ ਹੈ। ਕੋਈ ਬੁਖਾਰ, ਕੰਬਣੀ, ਜਾਂ ਭਾਰ ਘਟਣ ਦੀ ਰਿਪੋਰਟ ਨਹੀਂ ਹੈ। ਭੁੱਖ ਆਮ ਹੈ। ਮਰੀਜ਼ ਇੱਕ ਘਰੇਲੂ ਔਰਤ ਹੈ।

- sex / gender: Male
- age: 18
- marital_status: Never Married
- occupation: Stall and Market Salespersons, Other
- education_level: Matric/Secondary
- zone: Urban
- state: Punjab
- district: Moga

He is never married, has a matriculation education, and speaks Punjabi as his first language with functional Hindi. Salkhan lives in an urban zone and is single. Document format guide for Surgical / Operative Note (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Operative note — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|...]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|OT]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|50]] [[GENDER|Female]] Surgeon [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Procedure / findings: MUST describe an actual procedure/operation matching the
assigned clinical domain (not a psychiatry admission note). Keep relative names
gender-plausible.

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- ADMISSION_NUMBER — Admission Number e.g. [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
- WARD_NUMBER — Ward Number e.g. [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]
- BED_NUMBER — Bed Number e.g. [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- PATIENT_ID — Patient ID e.g. [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ਸਰਜੀਕਲ ਨੋਟ — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Civil Hospital Moga]] [[HOSPITAL_ID|CHM-001]] Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-MOG-001]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|OT]] [[BED_NUMBER|OT-1]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Salkhan Varma]] [[AGE|18]] [[GENDER|Male]] ਸਰਜਨ [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Gurpreet Singh]]
ਪ੍ਰਕਿਰਿਆ: ਖੱਬੇ ਟਿਬੀਆ ਅਤੇ ਫਿਬੁਲਾ ਦੀ ਓਪਨ ਰਿਡਕਸ਼ਨ ਐਂਡ ਇੰਟਰਨਲ ਫਿਕਸੇਸ਼ਨ (ORIF)।
ਮੌਜੂਦਾ ਬਿਮਾਰੀ ਦਾ ਇਤਿਹਾਸ: ਮਰੀਜ਼, 18 ਸਾਲਾ ਮਰਦ, ਇੱਕ ਰੇਹੜੀ-ਫੜੀ ਵਾਲਾ ਵਿਕਰੇਤਾ, ਮੋਟਰਸਾਈਕਲ ਹਾਦਸੇ ਤੋਂ ਬਾਅਦ ਐਮਰਜੈਂਸੀ ਵਿਭਾਗ ਵਿੱਚ ਲਿਆਂਦਾ ਗਿਆ। ਉਸ ਨੂੰ ਖੱਬੇ ਪੈਰ ਦੀ ਸੱਟ ਲੱਗੀ। ਪ੍ਰਕਿਰਿਆ ਲਈ ਸਹਿਮਤੀ ਉਸਦੇ ਪਿਤਾ, [[RELATIVE_NAME|Balwinder Varma]], ਤੋਂ ਲਈ ਗਈ, ਜੋ [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]] 'ਤੇ ਮੌਜੂਦ ਸੀ। ਮਰੀਜ਼ ਦਾ [[MRN|MRN-2024-MOG-001]] ਦੀ ਪੁਸ਼ਟੀ ਕੀਤੀ ਗਈ।
ਐਨਸਥੀਸੀਆ: ਜਨਰਲ।
ਨਤੀਜੇ: ਆਪਰੇਸ਼ਨ ਦੌਰਾਨ ਮਿਲੇ ਨਤੀਜਿਆਂ ਤੋਂ ਪਤਾ ਲੱਗਿਆ ਕਿ ਖੱਬੇ ਟਿਬੀਅਲ ਪਲੇਟੋ ਦਾ ਇੱਕ ਕਮਿਨਿਊਟਿਡ ਫ੍ਰੈਕਚਰ ਹੈ ਜਿਸ ਵਿੱਚ ਇੰਟਰਾ-ਆਰਟੀਕੂਲਰ ਐਕਸਟੈਂਸ਼ਨ ਹੈ ਅਤੇ ਫਿਬੁਲਰ ਸ਼ਾਫਟ ਦਾ ਇੱਕ ਵਿਸਥਾਪਿਤ ਤਿਰਛਾ ਫ੍ਰੈਕਚਰ ਹੈ।
ਪ੍ਰਕਿਰਿਆ: ਮਰੀਜ਼ ਨੂੰ ਇੱਕ ਰੇਡੀਓਲੂਸੈਂਟ ਟੇਬਲ 'ਤੇ ਸਿੱਧਾ ਲੇਟਾਇਆ ਗਿਆ। ਇੱਕ ਟੂਰਨੀਕੇਟ ਲਗਾਇਆ ਗਿਆ। ਫਿਬੁਲਾ ਦੇ ਉੱਪਰ ਇੱਕ ਪਾਸੇ ਦਾ ਚੀਰਾ ਲਗਾਇਆ ਗਿਆ, ਅਤੇ ਇੱਕ ਇੰਟਰਾਕੋਰਟੀਕਲ ਕਿੱਲ ਪਾਈ ਗਈ। ਟਿਬੀਅਲ ਪਲੇਟੋ ਲਈ ਇੱਕ ਮੀਡੀਅਲ ਪੈਰਾਪੈਟੇਲਰ ਪਹੁੰਚ ਦੀ ਵਰਤੋਂ ਕੀਤੀ ਗਈ। ਫਲੋਰੋਸਕੋਪਿਕ ਗਾਈਡੈਂਸ ਦੇ ਅਧੀਨ ਰਿਡਕਸ਼ਨ ਪ੍ਰਾਪਤ ਕੀਤੀ ਗਈ, ਅਤੇ ਟਿਬੀਆ 'ਤੇ ਇੱਕ ਲਾਕਿੰਗ ਪਲੇਟ ਲਗਾਈ ਗਈ। ਜ਼ਖ਼ਮ ਨੂੰ ਸਾਫ਼ ਕੀਤਾ ਗਿਆ ਅਤੇ ਪਰਤਾਂ ਵਿੱਚ ਬੰਦ ਕਰ ਦਿੱਤਾ ਗਿਆ।
ਆਪਰੇਸ਼ਨ ਤੋਂ ਬਾਅਦ ਦੀ ਯੋਜਨਾ: ਮਰੀਜ਼ ਨੂੰ 6 ਘੰਟੇ ਲਈ NPO ਰੱਖਿਆ ਜਾਵੇਗਾ। ਦਰਦ ਨਿਵਾਰਕ ਅਤੇ IV ਐਂਟੀਬਾਇਓਟਿਕਸ ਜਾਰੀ ਰੱਖੇ ਜਾਣਗੇ। ਇੱਕ ਨੀ-ਬਿਲੋ ਸਪਲਿੰਟ ਲਗਾਇਆ ਗਿਆ ਹੈ। ਮਰੀਜ਼ ਨੂੰ ਐਨਸਥੀਸੀਆ ਤੋਂ ਠੀਕ ਹੋਣ ਤੋਂ ਬਾਅਦ ਆਰਥੋਪੀਡਿਕ ਵਾਰਡ ਵਿੱਚ ਤਬਦੀਲ ਕੀਤਾ ਜਾਵੇਗਾ। 2 ਹਫ਼ਤਿਆਂ ਬਾਅਦ ਫਾਲੋ-ਅੱਪ। ਮਰੀਜ਼ [[PATIENT_ID|PID-MOG-2024-001]] ਸਥਿਰ ਹੈ।
payload.generated_text_en:
Operative note — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Civil Hospital Moga]] [[HOSPITAL_ID|CHM-001]] Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-MOG-001]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|OT]] [[BED_NUMBER|OT-1]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Salkhan Varma]] [[AGE|18]] [[GENDER|Male]] Surgeon [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Gurpreet Singh]]
Procedure: Open Reduction and Internal Fixation (ORIF) of left tibia and fibula.
History of Present Illness: The patient, an 18-year-old male market stall salesperson, was brought to the emergency department following a motorcycle accident. He sustained a left lower leg injury. Consent for the procedure was obtained from his father, [[RELATIVE_NAME|Balwinder Varma]], who was present at [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]. The patient's [[MRN|MRN-2024-MOG-001]] was verified.
Anesthesia: General.
Findings: Intra-operative findings revealed a comminuted fracture of the left tibial plateau with intra-articular extension and a displaced oblique fracture of the fibular shaft.
Procedure: The patient was positioned supine on a radiolucent table. A tourniquet was applied. A lateral incision was made over the fibula, and an intramedullary nail was inserted. A medial parapatellar approach was used for the tibial plateau. Reduction was achieved under fluoroscopic guidance, and a locking plate was applied to the tibia. The wound was irrigated and closed in layers.
Post-operative Plan: The patient will be kept NPO for 6 hours. Analgesics and IV antibiotics will be continued. A below-knee splint has been applied. The patient will be transferred to the orthopedic ward post-recovery from anesthesia. Follow-up in 2 weeks. Patient [[PATIENT_ID|PID-MOG-2024-001]] is stable.

payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).

  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):

GOOD:

You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.

ਮੁਲਾਕਾਤ ਦੀ ਤਾਰੀਖ: 15 ਮਈ 2024. ਮਰੀਜ਼ ਨੇ ਤਿੰਨ ਹਫ਼ਤਿਆਂ ਤੋਂ ਬਲਗਮ ਵਾਲੀ ਖੰਘ ਦੀ ਸ਼ਿਕਾਇਤ ਕੀਤੀ, ਜਿਸ ਦੇ ਨਾਲ ਸ਼ਾਮ ਨੂੰ ਹਲਕਾ ਬੁਖਾਰ ਹੁੰਦਾ ਸੀ। ਉਸਨੇ ਦੱਸਿਆ ਕਿ ਕਿਸੇ ਵੀ ਜਾਣੇ-ਪਛਾਣੇ TB ਮਰੀਜ਼ ਦੇ ਸੰਪਰਕ ਵਿੱਚ ਆਉਣ ਦਾ ਕੋਈ ਇਤਿਹਾਸ ਨਹੀਂ ਹੈ। NCD ਸਕ੍ਰੀਨਿੰਗ ਪ੍ਰੋਟੋਕੋਲ ਦੇ ਹਿੱਸੇ ਵਜੋਂ, ਉਸਦਾ ਬਲੱਡ ਪ੍ਰੈਸ਼ਰ 140/90 mmHg ਦਰਜ ਕੀਤਾ ਗਿਆ ਅਤੇ ਇੱਕ ਰੈਂਡਮ ਬਲੱਡ ਸ਼ੂਗਰ ਚੈੱਕ ਕੀਤੀ ਗਈ, ਜੋ ਕਿ 110 mg/dL ਸੀ। GeneXpert MTB/RIF ਟੈਸਟਿੰਗ ਲਈ ਇੱਕ ਬਲਗਮ ਦਾ ਨਮੂਨਾ ਇਕੱਠਾ ਕੀਤਾ ਗਿਆ ਸੀ। ਜੇਕਰ ਬਿਮਾਰੀ ਦੀ ਪੁਸ਼ਟੀ ਹੁੰਦੀ ਹੈ ਤਾਂ ਇਲਾਜ ਦਾ ਪੂਰਾ ਕੋਰਸ ਪੂਰਾ ਕਰਨ ਦੀ ਮਹੱਤਤਾ ਬਾਰੇ ਮਰੀਜ਼ ਨੂੰ ਸਲਾਹ ਦਿੱਤੀ ਗਈ ਸੀ। [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|Jaswinder Kaur]] ਨੂੰ ਖੇਤਰ ਵਿੱਚ ਫਾਲੋ-ਅੱਪ ਅਤੇ ਸੰਪਰਕ ਟਰੇਸਿੰਗ ਲਈ ਸੂਚਿਤ ਕੀਤਾ ਗਿਆ ਸੀ। ਸੰਚਾਰ ਲਈ ਮਰੀਜ਼ ਦਾ [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]] ਨੋਟ ਕੀਤਾ ਗਿਆ ਸੀ। ਉਸਦੇ [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-PB-JLR-2023-5678]] ਅਤੇ [[VOTER_ID|PBJ1234567]] ਵੇਰਵੇ ਫਾਈਲ ਵਿੱਚ ਹਨ। ਉਸਦਾ [[ABHA_ID|PB-1234-5678-9012]] ਇਸ ਮੁਲਾਕਾਤ ਨਾਲ ਜੋੜਿਆ ਗਿਆ ਹੈ। GeneXpert ਦੇ ਨਤੀਜਿਆਂ ਦੀ ਉਡੀਕ ਹੈ। ਜੇਕਰ ਪਾਜ਼ੀਟਿਵ ਹੈ, ਤਾਂ ਰਾਸ਼ਟਰੀ ਦਿਸ਼ਾ-ਨਿਰਦੇਸ਼ਾਂ ਅਨੁਸਾਰ Category I DOTS ਇਲਾਜ ਸ਼ੁਰੂ ਕਰੋ। ਇੱਕ ਹਫ਼ਤੇ ਵਿੱਚ ਫਾਲੋ-ਅੱਪ ਕਰੋ।

payload.culinary_persona: Dahiben Muchadiya delights in preparing traditional dishes like dhokla, thepla, and gathiya, favors tangy raw mango pickle and fresh coriander, enjoys the aroma of tempering mustard seeds in ghee while cooking, but rarely experiments beyond family recipes, preferring the comfort of familiar flavors.
payload.persona: Dahiben Muchadiya is a pragmatic farmer-mentor who fuses age-old juggad with modern sustainability, worries about cash flow, and can’t resist a good garba beat or a steaming plate of dhokla.
payload.cultural_background: Dahiben Muchadiya is rooted in the Gujarati agrarian culture of Sabar Kantha, a rural district in north‑west Gujarat. Her life revolves around the rhythms of the monsoon, the sowing and harvesting cycles, and the colourful festivals of western India – Navratri’s nine nights of Garba, the kite‑flying skies of Uttarayan, and community feasts that celebrate harvest bounty. The village social fabric is woven with joint‑family ties, caste‑based cooperatives, and a strong emphasis on self‑reliance (jugaad) in farming. While she respects age‑old customs, she also engages with government extension programmes and agricultural fairs, reflecting a blend of tradition and pragmatic openness.
payload.linguistic_background: She grew up speaking Gujarati at home and in the fields, and she can read and write Gujarati fluently thanks to her matric education. Hindi, taught in school as the second language, is her working language for official paperwork, market transactions, and communication with government officers; she understands and speaks it comfortably, though her reading of Hindi scripts is functional rather than literary. Her exposure to English is limited to basic school textbooks and occasional agricultural pamphlets, so she can comprehend simple agricultural terms in English but prefers Gujarati or Hindi for detailed discussions.
payload.skills_and_expertise: Dahiben possesses deep practical knowledge of crop cycles common to the semi‑arid regions of Sabar Kantha. She excels at planning sowing calendars, managing irrigation using both canal water and well pumps, and implementing integrated pest management with a mix of traditional neem‑based methods and modern chemical controls. Her expertise includes soil health assessment, seed selection, and supervising farm labourers, ensuring timely field operations. She is adept at maintaining simple financial records, negotiating procurement of fertilizers and seeds, and liaising with government extension officers for subsidy schemes. Her practical mindset also leverages innovative ‘jugaad’ solutions, such as repurposing old machinery for seed drilling.

payload.hobbies_and_interests: Dahiben finds relaxation in tending to her kitchen garden, where she cultivates seasonal vegetables and aromatic herbs. She enjoys participating in Garba dances during Navratri, a pastime that blends social interaction with cultural expression. Cooking traditional Gujarati dishes, especially dhokla and thepla, allows her to preserve family recipes. In quieter moments, she reads devotional texts and listens to folk bhajans on a small radio. Walking along the village lanes, often with her dog, provides a gentle form of exercise and helps relieve the stress of farm responsibilities.
payload.hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Kitchen gardening", "Garba dancing during Navratri", "Cooking Gujarati dishes", "Reading devotional texts", "Listening to folk bhajans", "Walking in the village", "Spending time with the family dog"]
payload.career_goals_and_ambitions: As she approaches her late fifties, Dahiben aims to modernise her family farm by gradually adopting sustainable practices such as drip irrigation and organic fertilisers, aiming to increase yield per hectare while reducing water usage. She aspires to mentor her younger relatives, especially her grandchildren, to take up progressive farming, and to secure a small enterprise that sells high‑value horticultural produce to nearby urban markets. Securing government subsidies for renewable energy use, and establishing a community demonstration plot for best practices are also part of her ambition. At a personal level, she wishes to ensure financial stability for her family and to be recognised locally as a knowledgeable farm overseer.

payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.

payload.travel_persona: Neelam Bala prefers well‑planned short trips to nearby hill stations like Shimla and Manali or cultural visits to Amritsar’s Golden Temple, balancing adventure with budget constraints and they often travel with a close circle of family friends.
payload.culinary_persona: Neelam Bala’s palate favors authentic regional flavors, regularly preparing sarson da saag, makki di roti, gur‑laden laddoos and herb‑infused tea with mint and coriander, while they enjoy experimenting with seasonal vegetables from their balcony garden.
payload.persona: Neelam Bala blends disciplined household management with a competitive streak, balancing meticulous budgeting and cooking while they occasionally worry‑driven over‑thinking.
payload.cultural_background: Neelam Bala belongs to the Punjabi Hindu community of Jalandhar. Growing up in an urban but tightly‑knit neighbourhood, she has been immersed in the vibrant customs of Punjab – from festive celebrations like Lohri and Diwali to the warmth of joint‑family gatherings. Her cultural upbringing emphasizes respect for elders, a love for Punjabi folk music and dance, and a strong sense of duty towards the household. These influences, combined with the practical, no‑nonsense ethos of city life, shape her preference for orderly routines, dependable habits, and a focus on tangible outcomes.
payload.linguistic_background: Neelam’s mother tongue is Punjabi, spoken in the Gurmukhi script, and she uses it for daily communication with family and neighbours. She is also fluent in Hindi, which she reads and writes using Devanagari, mainly for interacting with the wider community and accessing basic services. Her formal schooling ended before primary level, so her literacy is limited to simple reading and writing in both languages, and she has only a rudimentary grasp of English, sufficient for recognizing common words on product labels.
payload.skills_and_expertise: Neelam is adept at managing a household efficiently, budgeting monthly expenses, and planning meals for her family. She excels in preparing authentic Punjabi dishes such as sarson da saag, makki di roti, and various tiffin items. Her craftsmanship includes traditional Phulkari embroidery and basic sewing, which she occasionally uses for repairing clothing. Despite limited formal schooling, she possesses basic literacy in Punjabi (Gurmukhi) and Hindi (Devanagari) and can handle simple correspondence. She also has rudimentary computer skills, enough to use a basic spreadsheet for tracking household finances.

payload.hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Reading Punjabi literature", "Gardening", "Traditional cooking", "Embroidery (Phulkari)", "Listening to devotional music", "Yoga and meditation"]

payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete Surgical / Operative Note for clinical domain Maternal Health.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Punjabi (code=pa, script=Gurmukhi). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Maternal Health — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
MATERNAL SAFETY: clinical content must be age-plausible for persona age=29. Routine ANC/delivery typically ages 18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC/PNC/contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy as a routine story.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Punjab, district=Jalandhar.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Gurmukhi for Punjabi.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 29
- marital_status: Never Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Below Primary
- zone: Urban
- state: Punjab
- district: Jalandhar
- first_language: Punjabi
- clinical_persona_summary: A 29-year-old female from Jalandhar, Punjab, who is never married, has below-primary education, and lives in an urban zone. She is a homemaker fluent in Punjabi and Hindi, managing household duties with disciplined budgeting and occasional over-thinking. Her lifestyle includes daily walks and yoga, and she follows Punjabi cultural customs like Lohri and Diwali.

Document format guide for Surgical / Operative Note (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Operative note — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|...]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|OT]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|50]] [[GENDER|Female]] Surgeon [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Procedure / findings: MUST describe an actual procedure/operation matching the
assigned clinical domain (not a psychiatry admission note). Keep relative names
gender-plausible.

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- ADMISSION_NUMBER — Admission Number e.g. [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
- WARD_NUMBER — Ward Number e.g. [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]
- BED_NUMBER — Bed Number e.g. [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- PATIENT_ID — Patient ID e.g. [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ਪ੍ਰਕਿਰਿਆ / ਨਤੀਜੇ:
ਭਰੂਣ ਦੀ ਤਕਲੀਫ਼ ਲਈ 03:45 ਵਜੇ ਐਮਰਜੈਂਸੀ ਲੋਅਰ ਸੈਗਮੈਂਟ ਸਿਸੇਰੀਅਨ ਸੈਕਸ਼ਨ ਕੀਤਾ ਗਿਆ। ਮਰੀਜ਼ ਨੇ 8 ਘੰਟੇ ਲਈ ਨਿਯਮਤ ਸੁੰਗੜਨ ਅਤੇ ਪਾਣੀ ਫਟਣ ਨਾਲ ਪੇਸ਼ ਕੀਤਾ। ਜਾਂਚ 'ਤੇ, ਗਰਭ ਦਾ ਮੂੰਹ 4cm ਫੈਲਿਆ ਹੋਇਆ ਸੀ, ਸਟੇਸ਼ਨ -1। ਭਰੂਣ ਦੀ ਦਿਲ ਦੀ ਗਤੀ ਵਿੱਚ ਦੇਰੀ ਨਾਲ ਗਿਰਾਵਟ ਦਿਖਾਈ ਦਿੱਤੀ। ਤੁਰੰਤ ਜਣੇਪੇ ਲਈ ਫੈਸਲਾ ਲਿਆ ਗਿਆ।

ਆਪਰੇਟਿਵ ਨਤੀਜੇ:
ਗਰਭਾਸ਼ਯ ਵਧਿਆ ਹੋਇਆ, ਹੇਠਲਾ ਹਿੱਸਾ ਪਤਲਾ ਹੋ ਗਿਆ, ਕੋਈ ਪਲੇਸੈਂਟਾ ਪ੍ਰੀਵੀਆ ਨਹੀਂ ਸੀ।
ਨਰ ਬੱਚਾ 04:15 ਵਜੇ ਪੈਦਾ ਹੋਇਆ, ਭਾਰ 3.2kg, ਐਪਗਾਰ 8/9।
ਪਲੇਸੈਂਟਾ ਆਪਣੇ ਆਪ ਬਾਹਰ ਆ ਗਿਆ।
ਗਰਭਾਸ਼ਯ ਚੰਗੀ ਤਰ੍ਹਾਂ ਸੁੰਗੜਿਆ ਹੋਇਆ ਸੀ।
ਅਨੁਮਾਨਿਤ ਖੂਨ ਦਾ ਨੁਕਸਾਨ 250ml ਸੀ।
ਮਰੀਜ਼ ਪੂਰੀ ਪ੍ਰਕਿਰਿਆ ਦੌਰਾਨ ਸਥਿਰ ਰਿਹਾ।
ਆਪਰੇਸ਼ਨ ਤੋਂ ਬਾਅਦ: ਨਿਰੀਖਣ ਲਈ [[BED_NUMBER|12]] ਵਿੱਚ [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]] ਤਬਦੀਲ ਕੀਤਾ ਗਿਆ।
[[RELATIVE_NAME|Jaswinder Singh]] (ਪਿਤਾ) ਸੂਚਿਤ ਕੀਤਾ ਗਿਆ ਅਤੇ ਮੌਜੂਦ ਸੀ।
